name: ipinfo
documentation:
The official Python library for IPinfo.

IPinfo prides itself on being the most reliable, accurate, and in-depth source of IP address data available anywhere.
We process terabytes of data to produce our custom IP geolocation, company, carrier and IP type data sets.
You can visit our developer docs at https://ipinfo.io/developers.

modules.name: ipinfo.handler_utils
documentation: Utilities used in handlers.

functions.name: ipinfo.handler_utils.get_headers
documentation: Build headers for request to IPinfo API.

functions.name: ipinfo.handler_utils.format_details
documentation: Format details given a countries object.

functions.name: ipinfo.handler_utils.read_coords
documentation:
Given a location of the form `<lat>,<lon>`, returns the latitude and
longitude as a tuple.

Returns None for each tuple item if the form is invalid.

functions.name: ipinfo.handler_utils.return_or_fail
documentation: Either throws `e` if `raise_on_fail` or else returns `v`.

functions.name: ipinfo.handler_utils.cache_key
documentation: Transforms a user-input key into a versioned cache key.

modules.name: ipinfo.exceptions
documentation: Exceptions thrown by the IPinfo service.

classes.name: ipinfo.exceptions.RequestQuotaExceededError
documentation: Error indicating that users monthly request quota has been passed.

classes.name: ipinfo.exceptions.TimeoutExceededError
documentation: Error indicating that some timeout has been exceeded.

modules.name: ipinfo.details
documentation: Details returned by the IPinfo service.

classes.name: ipinfo.details.Details

methods.name: ipinfo.details.Details.__init__
documentation: Initialize by settings `details` attribute.

methods.name: ipinfo.details.Details.__getattr__
documentation: Return attribute if it exists in details array, else return error.

methods.name: ipinfo.details.Details.all
documentation: Return all details as dict.
Encapsulates data for single IP address.

functions.name: ipinfo.getHandler
documentation: Create and return Handler object.

functions.name: ipinfo.getHandlerAsync
documentation: Create an return an asynchronous Handler object.

modules.name: ipinfo.cache.interface
documentation: Abstract interface for caching IPinfo data.

classes.name: ipinfo.cache.interface.CacheInterface
documentation: Interface for using custom cache.

modules.name: ipinfo.cache.default
documentation: A default cache implementation that uses `cachetools` for an in-memory LRU cache.

classes.name: ipinfo.cache.default.DefaultCache
documentation: Default, in-memory cache.

modules.name: ipinfo.handler_async
documentation: Main API client asynchronous handler for fetching data from the IPinfo service.

classes.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler

methods.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Handler object with country name list and the
cache initialized.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler.init
documentation:
Initializes internal aiohttp connection pool.

This isn't _required_, as the pool is initialized lazily when needed.
But in case you require non-lazy initialization, you may await this.

This is idempotent.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler.deinit
documentation:
Deinitialize the async handler.

This is required in case you need to let go of the memory/state
associated with the async handler in a long-running process.

This is idempotent.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler.getDetails
documentation: Get details for specified IP address as a Details object.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler.getBatchDetails
documentation:
Get details for a batch of IP addresses at once.

There is no specified limit to the number of IPs this function can
accept; it can handle as much as the user can fit in RAM (along with
all of the response data, which is at least a magnitude larger than the
input list).

The input list is broken up into batches to abide by API requirements.
The batch size can be adjusted with `batch_size` but is clipped to
`BATCH_MAX_SIZE`.
Defaults to `BATCH_MAX_SIZE`.

For each batch, `timeout_per_batch` indicates the maximum seconds to
spend waiting for the HTTP request to complete. If any batch fails with
this timeout, the whole operation fails.
Defaults to `BATCH_REQ_TIMEOUT_DEFAULT` seconds.

`timeout_total` is a seconds-denominated hard-timeout for the time
spent in HTTP operations; regardless of whether all batches have
succeeded so far, if `timeout_total` is reached, the whole operation
will fail by raising `TimeoutExceededError`.
Defaults to being turned off.

`raise_on_fail`, if turned off, will return any result retrieved so far
rather than raise an exception when errors occur, including timeout and
quota errors.
Defaults to on.

The concurrency level is currently unadjustable; coroutines will be
created and consumed for all batches at once.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler._do_batch_req
documentation: Coroutine which will do the actual POST request for getBatchDetails.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler_async.AsyncHandler._ensure_aiohttp_ready
documentation: Ensures aiohttp internal state is initialized.

Allows client to request data for specified IP address asynchronously.
Instantiates and maintains access to cache.

modules.name: ipinfo.handler
documentation: Main API client handler for fetching data from the IPinfo service.

classes.name: ipinfo.handler.Handler

methods.name: ipinfo.handler.Handler.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Handler object with country name list and the
cache initialized.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler.Handler.getDetails
documentation:
Get details for specified IP address as a Details object.

If `timeout` is not `None`, it will override the client-level timeout
just for this operation.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler.Handler.getBatchDetails
documentation:
Get details for a batch of IP addresses at once.

There is no specified limit to the number of IPs this function can
accept; it can handle as much as the user can fit in RAM (along with
all of the response data, which is at least a magnitude larger than the
input list).

The input list is broken up into batches to abide by API requirements.
The batch size can be adjusted with `batch_size` but is clipped to
`BATCH_MAX_SIZE`.
Defaults to `BATCH_MAX_SIZE`.

For each batch, `timeout_per_batch` indicates the maximum seconds to
spend waiting for the HTTP request to complete. If any batch fails with
this timeout, the whole operation fails.
Defaults to `BATCH_REQ_TIMEOUT_DEFAULT` seconds.

`timeout_total` is a seconds-denominated hard-timeout for the time
spent in HTTP operations; regardless of whether all batches have
succeeded so far, if `timeout_total` is reached, the whole operation
will fail by raising `TimeoutExceededError`.
Defaults to being turned off.

`raise_on_fail`, if turned off, will return any result retrieved so far
rather than raise an exception when errors occur, including timeout and
quota errors.
Defaults to on.

methods.name: ipinfo.handler.Handler.getMap
documentation:
Gets a URL to a map on https://ipinfo.io/map given a list of IPs (max
500,000).

Allows client to request data for specified IP address.
Instantiates and maintains access to cache.
